# ccsidekick

> A Claude Code status line with a reactive ASCII character and a full widget layer. Zero token spend, no Claude API, local-first, MIT. Cost, git, and usage read straight from your Claude Code transcripts.

ccsidekick replaces Claude Code's status line with a character (Batman, Spider-Man, Yoda, and 15 more) that reacts to your session and warms to you over time, next to a real status line of 33 toggleable widgets across 75+ themes. It never calls the Claude API and spends zero tokens; all state lives on disk under `~/.claude/`.

## Install

- `npx ccsidekick` runs the guided setup wizard (character, theme, comments) and wires it into Claude Code's settings.
- `npx ccsidekick setup --character spiderman --theme houston --mode fixed` configures and wires everything from flags, with no TUI (good for scripts and agents).

## Key facts

- 18 bundled characters, 75+ themes, 33 widgets, all shipped with no install/browse/download step.
- Zero token spend, no Claude API, and no network on the render path (local-first; state on disk under `~/.claude/`).
- Cost is transcript-derived in-house and deduped globally by `(message.id, requestId)`, which fixes Claude Code's resumed-session over-count (its own `cost.total_cost_usd` double-counts replayed context).
- Character packs are pure data (`pack.json`), never executed code; each pack ships its own theme.
- Two binaries: `ccsidekick` (setup/TUI) and `ccsidekick-render` (the hot render path, which pulls in no React/Ink).
- Requires Node 20.6+ (the render path runs under plain Node; Bun is the dev toolchain only); MIT licensed.

## What makes it different

- A reactive, in-character figure that comments on session events and warms over time. No other Claude Code status line has this.
- Correct cost: the global `(message.id, requestId)` dedup fixes the over-count Claude Code reports on resumed sessions.
- Breadth in the box: 18 characters, 75+ themes, 33 widgets, no download step.

## FAQ

- Does it use the Claude API or spend tokens? No. Zero token spend, no Claude API; cost is read from your local transcripts.
- Is my data private? Yes, local-first. The only network use is an optional weekly currency-rate refresh and an optional account-usage lookup, both off by default, and neither on the render path. The account-usage lookup, when you turn it on, sends your Anthropic OAuth token to Anthropic's account-usage endpoint.
- How do I install it? Run `npx ccsidekick` for the wizard, or `npx ccsidekick setup` with flags for a non-interactive install.
- Can I add my own character? Yes; packs are pure data, and the pack-author path documents building one.
